---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size40[

Final Exam Paper

1. Review your cases

2. Introduce the prompt

]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for class
1. Prep email with link to Google shared document for collecting citations



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size60[**Assignment for Monday**]

.size45[
Bring to class a recent international political event we have not explored in class. 

Find something you think is interesting and be ready to explain to the class why you picked it.
]

???

HAS EVERYBODY SUBMITTED THEIR APA CITATION TO THE SHARED DOCUMENT?

Everybody skim through them and make sure all are properly formatted.

Just flag any citations that might need cleaning up.

*Submitters must correct any errors*



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size70[**Introduce the Cases**]

.size60[
1. Why is this .textred[**international**]?

2. Why is this .textred[**political**]?

3. Why do you find it interesting?
]

???

I'll ask each of you to present your case as an answer to these three questions

Everybody take a couple minutes to reflect on their case and make sure you have a solid answer for them.

<br>

Ok, let's hear about our cases.

<br>

Alright, we have our cases, now let's talk about the final exam prompt.



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Final Exam (Due May 11, 3pm)**]

.size40[
Analyze a recent international political event using three of the theories, models or causal mechanisms we've studied in class this term. 

+ Make a convincing argument
	+ (e.g. logical, clear, credible and critical)

+ Evidence: Class material and beyond (as needed)
]

???

QUESTIONS ON THE ASSIGNMENT?

- Due by end of final exam window (May 11, 3pm)

- Bibliography



---

class: middle, center, slideblue

.size60[**Assignment for Wednesday**]

.size45[
Review your class notes and identify three theories, models or mechanisms we studied this term that could each provide a useful explanation of your event. 

Come to class with your three choices (e.g. premises) an an outline for your final paper.
]

???

Assignment for Wednesday
